title: dianthus pungens subsp. hispanicus
intro: Dianthus pungens subsp. hispanicus, also known as the Iberian pink, is a flowering plant species in the carnation family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dianthus pungens subsp. hispanicus include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, and poultices to leverage its medicinal properties.
